title: An object with integral type or pointer to void type shall not be converted to an object with pointer type
description: An object with integral type or pointer to void type shall not be converted to an object with pointer typeAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode Smellmisra-c++2008Why is this an issue?More InfoThis rule is a strict implementation of a MISRA (Motor Industry Software Reliability Association) rule. MISRA defines best practices for developing safety-critical software. You can learn more about this rule in the MISRA documents referenced below.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
int value = 42;
char* ptr = (char*)value; // Noncompliant: converting integral to pointer

void* voidPtr = malloc(100);
int* intPtr = (int*)voidPtr; // Noncompliant: converting void* to specific pointer type
compliant:
int value = 42;
int* ptr = &value; // Compliant: taking address, not converting value

int array[10];
int* intPtr = array; // Compliant: array-to-pointer conversion

checks.message: Avoid converting integral types or void pointers to object pointer types - use proper type casting or avoid the conversion
